title: Spain: No Extensions for Crypto Firms to Meet EU MiCA Deadline
summary: Spain's financial regulator has ruled out any extensions for crypto companies to comply with the EU's MiCA regulations. This means firms must secure proper licensing by the deadline or face penalties. The deadline is July 2026.
body:
Spain’s financial regulator, Carlos San Basilio, has made it clear that there will be no exceptions or extensions for crypto exchanges to meet the EU’s Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) compliance deadline. The MiCA regulations, set to take full effect in July 2026, require all crypto exchanges offering services to EU-based users to be properly licensed. 

This firm stance means that any crypto companies still operating without the necessary licenses by the deadline will face legal consequences. The MiCA regulations aim to create a unified regulatory framework for crypto assets across the European Union, ensuring consumer protection and market stability. 

For everyday users, this ensures that the crypto exchanges they use will be held to higher standards of transparency and security. It also means that users should be cautious about using platforms that might not comply with these regulations, as they could face disruptions or legal issues. 

Crypto companies operating in the EU should prioritize obtaining their MiCA licenses as soon as possible. Users should also keep an eye on announcements from their preferred exchanges regarding their compliance status. This is a critical step toward a more regulated and secure crypto market in Europe.
twitterThread: Spain’s financial regulator just shut down any hopes of extensions for crypto firms to meet the EU’s MiCA deadline. No exceptions, no delays.
This means all crypto exchanges serving EU users must be licensed by July 2026. No wiggle room. The goal? A safer, more transparent market for everyone.

If you’re using a crypto exchange, check if they’re compliant. Non-compliant platforms could face penalties. Stay informed → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-26-spain-no-extensions-for-crypto-firms-to-meet-eu-mica-deadline
